2026-04-10T07:08:29.1291724Z ````markdown
2026-04-10T07:08:30.3814617Z ## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage
2026-04-10T07:08:30.3815035Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:31.6619845Z **Summary:** The author's `Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0` agent creation flow is failing with HTTP 409 Conflict on `CreateAgentVersionAsync` and HTTP 404 Not Found on `DeleteAgentAsync`, leaving orphaned agents that don't appear in the Foundry UI.
2026-04-10T07:08:31.6621687Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:31.6621856Z <details>
2026-04-10T07:08:31.6642621Z <summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>
2026-04-10T07:08:31.6642995Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:31.6643179Z **Package:** `Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0`  
2026-04-10T07:08:32.9066774Z **Affected API:** `AgentsClient.CreateAgentVersionAsync`, `AgentsClient.DeleteAgentAsync`  
2026-04-10T07:08:32.9068296Z **Scenarios:**
2026-04-10T07:08:32.9079383Z - Calling `CreateAgentVersionAsync` returns `409 Conflict: "The resource already exists or was modified concurrently. Please retry."`
2026-04-10T07:08:34.0117469Z - Calling `DeleteAgentAsync("WebSearchAgent")` returns `404 Not Found: "Agent not found"`
2026-04-10T07:08:34.0127080Z - Agents not visible in Microsoft Foundry UI, so manual cleanup is impossible
2026-04-10T07:08:34.0127874Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:35.2398774Z **Root ask:** Understand why agent creation fails with 409 and why deletion fails with 404, and how to recover from this stuck state.
2026-04-10T07:08:35.2399553Z </details>
2026-04-10T07:08:35.2399689Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:35.2399786Z <details>
2026-04-10T07:08:35.3012694Z <summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>
2026-04-10T07:08:35.3013282Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:36.5399549Z The 409 suggests a resource with the same name/version already exists server-side (potentially a partial/stale state not reflected in the UI). The 404 on delete suggests the agent name-based lookup is not finding the resource, possibly because the resource uses an internal ID rather than display name.
2026-04-10T07:08:36.5401017Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:36.5401436Z **Suggested investigation steps:**
2026-04-10T07:08:37.7386912Z 1. Try listing agents via `AgentsClient.GetAgentsAsync()` to check if the agent exists server-side with a different ID or state than expected.
2026-04-10T07:08:39.4395554Z 2. Attempt deletion using the agent's `id` (GUID) rather than the display name `"WebSearchAgent"`, in case `DeleteAgentAsync` requires the resource ID.
2026-04-10T07:08:40.2978675Z 3. Check whether the 409 conflict is caused by a prior failed/partial creation — inspect whether retrying with a different agent name/version resolves the issue.
2026-04-10T07:08:40.2987978Z </details>
2026-04-10T07:08:40.2988176Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:40.2988305Z <details>
2026-04-10T07:08:40.2988792Z <summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>
2026-04-10T07:08:40.2989438Z 
2026-04-10T07:08:41.7457957Z - **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.Projects` is a data plane client library under `/sdk/ai/`; this is an SDK-level API behavior issue, not a service REST API bug.
2026-04-10T07:08:41.7465719Z - **Service:** `AI Projects` — The package explicitly named is `Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0`, which maps directly to the `%AI Projects` service label.

2026-04-10T07:08:51.4508749Z

2026-04-10T07:08:51.4509800Z   
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4510134Z   **Describe the bug:**
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4511274Z   The reporter is implementing an agent with a web search tool against a Microsoft Foundry instance. Previously working code started failing with two errors:
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4512486Z   
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4513592Z   1. `HTTP 409 Conflict` — `"The resource already exists or was modified concurrently. Please retry."` — when calling `CreateAgentVersionAsync`.
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4515289Z   2. `HTTP 404 Not Found` — `"Agent not found"` — when calling `DeleteAgentAsync` with the agent name.
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4516095Z   
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4516902Z   The agents are not visible in the Microsoft Foundry UI, so they cannot be manually deleted either.
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4517746Z   
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4518073Z   **Expected behavior:**
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4518639Z   - `CreateAgentVersionAsync` should create a new agent version.
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4519650Z   - `DeleteAgentVersionAsync` / `DeleteAgentAsync` should successfully delete agent versions.
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4520627Z   
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4520942Z   **Actual behavior:**
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4521492Z   - `CreateAgentVersionAsync` → `409 Conflict`
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4522243Z   - `DeleteAgentAsync("WebSearchAgent")` → `404 Not Found`
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4522787Z   
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4524093Z   **Reproduction Steps:** Provided (C# code using `AIProjectClient`, `DeclarativeAgentDefinition`, `ResponseTool.CreateWebSearchTool`, `CreateAgentVersionAsync`, `DeleteAgentAsync`).
2026-04-10T07:08:51.4525707Z

2026-04-10T07:08:51.4663895Z   **Summary:** The author's `Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0` agent creation flow is failing with HTTP 409 Conflict on `CreateAgentVersionAsync` and HTTP 404 Not Found on `DeleteAgentAsync`, leaving orphaned agents that don't appear in the Foundry UI.

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters).
